---
title: "Extending the model"
format: revealjs
---

## Birnbaum revisited

:::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../shared_media/images/date_night_small.png){height=500}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

- Does playing hard to get work?^[[Birnbaum et al. (2020). *Journal of Social and Personal Relationships*. Study 3.]( https://doi.org/10.1177/0265407520927469)]
- Heterosexual participants conversed with an opposite-sex confederate over Instant
Messenger for 8 mins
- **Interest**: Final message coded for the number of expressions of romantic interest (range 0 to 4)
- **Hard to get**: 3 items rated 1 (not at all) and 5 (very much so)
  - *The other participant is hard to get*
- **Mate value**: 4 items rated 1 (not at all) and 5 (very much so)
  - *I perceive the other participant as a valued mate*

:::
:::

## The model

::: center-h
::: txt_mulberry
$$
\text{interest}_i = \hat{b}_0 + \hat{b}_1\text{hard to get}_i +e_i
$$
:::
:::


```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false

# hard_tib <- readr::read_csv("data/birnbaum.csv")

hard_tib <- haven::read_sav("data/birnbaum_study_3.sav", col_select = c(
  "QualtricsID", "MCoding", "MHardtoGet", "MMateValue"
)) |> 
  dplyr::rename(
    id = QualtricsID,
    interest = MCoding,
    hard_to_get = MHardtoGet,
    mate_value = MMateValue
  ) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(
    mean_interest = mean(interest)
  )

hard_lm <- lm(interest ~ hard_to_get, data = hard_tib)
more_hard_lm <- lm(interest ~ hard_to_get + mate_value, data = hard_tib)
more_hard_null <- lm(mean_interest ~ hard_to_get + mate_value, data = hard_tib)
```


:::: columns
::: {.column width="40%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 3

birm_gg <- ggplot2::ggplot(hard_tib, aes(x = hard_to_get, y = interest)) +
  geom_point(size = 1, colour = mulberry, alpha = 0.6) +
  stat_smooth(method = "lm", colour = blue, fill = blue) +
  labs(y = "Expressions of interest (0-4)", x = "Perception that stooge was hard to get (1-5)") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, 5), ylim = c(0, 4)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 01:5) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:4) +
  theme_minimal()
birm_gg
```
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
::: tbl_s
```{r}
#| echo: false

birm_noci_tbl <- model_parameters(hard_lm) 
display(birm_noci_tbl)
```
:::
:::
:::

\

::: {.callout-caution icon = false}
## {{< fa brain >}} Think about it!

- How do we extend the model to include mate value?
:::


## Extending the linear model


::: center-h
::: txt_mulberry
::: txt_l
$$
\text{interest}_i = \hat{b}_0 + \hat{b}_1\text{hard to get}_i + \hat{b}_2\text{mate value}_i +e_i
$$
:::
:::
:::

::: center-h

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5


s3d <- scatterplot3d::scatterplot3d(x = hard_tib$hard_to_get,
                               y = hard_tib$mate_value,
                               z = hard_tib$interest,
                               color = alpha(blue, 0.6),
                               angle = 10,
                               pch = 16,
                               zlim = c(0, 4),
                               xlim = c(1, 5),
                               ylim = c(1, 5),
                               box = FALSE,
                               cex.symbols = 2,
                               ylab = "Mate value (1-5)",
                               zlab = "Interest (0-4)",
                               xlab = "Hard to get (1-5)"
                               )

s3d$plane3d(more_hard_lm, lty = 2, draw_polygon = TRUE, col = alpha(mulberry, 0.3), polygon_args = list(col = alpha(mulberry, 0.1), border = mulberry))
```

:::

## Parameter estimates

::: center-h
::: txt_mulberry
::: txt_l
$\hat{\text{interest}}_i = -0.49 + 0.13\text{ hard to get}_i + 0.39\text{ mate value}_i$
:::
:::
:::

::: center-h
::: tbl_s
```{r}
#| echo: false

more_hard_tbl <- model_parameters(more_hard_lm) 
display(more_hard_tbl)
```
:::
:::

::: fragmet

* As the perception that the other person was hard to get increased by 1 (on a scale from 1-5), **`r value_from_ez(more_hard_tbl, row = 2)`** more expressions of interest were made (when mate value is constant)
  + This effect is not significant, `r report_pe(more_hard_tbl, row = 2)`
  + This is the effect of 'hard to get' on interest **adjusted for** the effect of 'mate value'

:::
::: fragmet

* As the perception of mate value increased by 1 (on a scale from 1-5), **`r value_from_ez(more_hard_tbl, row = 3)`** more expressions of interest were made (when perceptions of being hard to get are constant)
  + This effect is significant, `r report_pe(more_hard_tbl, row = 3)`
  - This is the effect of 'mate value' on interest **adjusted for** the effect of 'hard to get'

:::



## How to enter predictors

::: fragment

- Hierarchical
  - Experimenter decides the order in which variables are entered into the model
  - Best for theory testing

:::
::: fragment

- Forced entry
  - All predictors are entered simultaneously

:::
::: fragment

- Stepwise
  - Predictors are selected using their semi-partial correlation with the outcome
  - Can produce spurious results
  - Use only for exploratory analysis
 
::: 
  
# When a hierarchical model gets you dressed

## {background-video="media/arlo_hierarchical_small.mp4" background-size="cover"}


# When a stepwise model gets you dressed

## {background-video="media/arlo_stepwise_small.mp4" background-size="cover"}

## {background-video="media/arlo_outtake_small.mp4" background-size="cover"}
